concept: Access / Reach, Receive, or Use Under Stated Conditions
description: To reach or enter a place, or to receive and use a resource, communication, service, or activity under stated conditions. Access is a practical relation, so an open door may still lead to a route a person cannot use; 'ruweli' names reaching or use, while 'pawela' (participate) begins when someone takes part.
sound_symbolism: Deep 'ru' starts with a rolling tongue. Stressed 'we' moves the contact to rounded lips and glides into balanced 'e', while final 'li' returns to a lighter liquid and bright 'i'. Two kinds of open motion join in a steady rise and finish with a free liquid release.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'mia ha shoeka ruweli.' (I access this route.) The object may be a place, resource, communication, service, or activity. The event-noun rule supplies an act or condition of access. Distinct from 'koema' (enter), 'howela' (receive), and 'pawela' (participate).

pillars.solarpunk-values: A shared garden is not available merely because it exists; 'ruweli' asks whether a person can reach and use it under actual conditions.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The verb lets a speaker locate blocked access in a practical relation instead of turning the person who encounters the block into the problem.

tags.activity: reaching or using a stated activity or resource
tags.community: practical entry into shared services and processes
tags.spatial: access to a place or route under stated conditions
